LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON AND HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION: GRANTED. THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION, EXCEPT THAT THE COURT RETAINS JURISDICTION TO HEAR AND DECIDE DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7, WHICH DEFENDANTS HAVE INFORMED THE COURT THEY INTEND TO FILE THIS WEEK. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON, HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF FILED AND MAINTAINED THIS ACTION PRIMARILY FOR AN IMPROPER PURPOSE WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 128.7(B). (COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER MIRRORING THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL TO COURT- COPYING OPPOSING COUNSEL- FOR SIGNATURE). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

RESPONSE TO DEFS' PETITION TO CONFIRM AWARD FILED BY PLAINTIFF LAI, DAVID

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON AND HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. THE PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS NOT PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE. PLAINTIFF CITES TO NO LEGAL AUTHORITY IN SUPPORT OF ITS ARGUMENT THAT DEFENDANTS ARE REQUIRED TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT ON A MATTER THAT HAS BEEN ARBITRATED. " ' "[A] POINT WHICH IS MERELY SUGGESTED BY [A PARTY'S] COUNSEL, WITH NO SUPPORTING ARGUMENT OR AUTHORITY, IS DEEMED TO BE WITHOUT FOUNDATION AND REQUIRES NO DISCUSSION." [CITATION.]' [CITATION.]" (DO IT URSELF MOVING & STORAGE, INC. V. BROWN, LEIFER, SLATKIN & BERNS (1992) 7 CAL.APP.4TH 27, 35, SUPERSEDED BY STATUTE ON OTHER GROUNDS IN UNION BANK V. SUP. CT. (1995) 31 CAL.APP.4TH 573, 582.) THE USE OF JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORMS IS OPTIONAL AND THEIR USE IS NOT A BAR TO RECOVERY. (VAPNEK, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: PROF'L RESPONSIBILITY (RUTTER) 5:1143A.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-31-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arb Award
Off Calendar Jan-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALLISON LANE COOPER, ESQ., 415.957.3045, FOR DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON AND HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON AND HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award IS Granted. The petition to confirm arbitration award is not procedurally defective. Plaintiff cites to no legal authority in support of its argument that Defendants are required to file a cross-complaint on a matter that has been arbitrated. " ' "[A] point which is merely suggested by [a party's] counsel, with no supporting argument or authority, is deemed to be without foundation and requires no discussion." [Citation.]' [Citation.]" (Do It Urself Moving & Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 35, superseded by statute on other grounds in Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 582.) The use of Judicial Council forms is optional and their use is not a bar to recovery. (Vapnek, Cal. Practice Guide: Prof'l Responsibility (Rutter) 5:1143a.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MERRILL EMERICK, ESQ., ANDERLINI & EMERICK LLP, 650.212.0001, FOR PLAINTIFF DAVID LAI.
ALLISON LANE COOPER, ESQ., KKSRR, 415.249.8330, FOR DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON AND HANSON BRIDGETT LLP.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON, HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP'S Motion For Sanctions is denied. Defendants fail to demonstrate that plaintiff filed and maintained this action primarily for an improper purpose within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 128.7(b). (counsel for plaintiff to prepare a form of order mirroring the court's ruling and email to court- copying opposing counsel- for signature). Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Ntc Of Prtition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: appearances: jennifer r. mcglone, esq., krieg, keller, sloan, reilley & roman, 415.249.8330, for defendantS richard stratton AND HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP; no other appearances.

Judge Kahn discloses that, while he does not believe he has ever had any contact with Mr. Stratton, more than eleven years ago he had contact with Mr. Stratton's wife in connection with her role as the client representative for a lawsuit where Judge Kahn served as the client's attorney. Judge Kahn does not believe that this contact affects his ability to be fair and impartial in this case or require that he recuse himself.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification- DEFENDANTS RICHARD STRATTON AND HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP'S Petition To Compel Arbitration: Granted. The case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration, except that the court retains jurisdiction to hear and decide defendants' motion for sanctions pursuant to california code of civil procedure section 128.7, which defendants have informed the court they intend to file this week. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella; Not reported
